public_goals: Keep reimbursement workable for necessary child expenses.
Avoid a process that feels like one parent can unilaterally refuse repayment.
Reduce repeated arguments over documentation and response timing.
private_concerns: Feels some necessary expenses get second-guessed after the fact.
Wants a process that protects fairness without inviting endless delay.
Does not want reimbursement discussions to turn into a blame contest.
red_lines: Will not accept a reimbursement process that effectively gives one parent veto power after an expense is incurred.
Will not accept necessary child expenses being ignored because the communication process is too rigid.
emotional_triggers: necessary child expenses treated as optional favors
documentation requests used like a veto after the fact
disclosure_tendencies: provides more detail when reimbursement rules are framed as workable rather than punitive
response_to_perceived_bias_or_pressure: pushes back if the process sounds like unilateral approval control
becomes more cooperative when notice and reimbursement are framed as mutual responsibilities
starting_positions: Primary position: repayment should stay workable for necessary child expenses if notice and documentation are provided promptly.
Secondary position: expense coordination should not become one-sided approval control.
likely_openings: I do not want every reimbursement to turn into a second argument.
If we have a process, it needs to be workable and fair for both of us.
